verses.text: The payment due!
What is the payment due?
And what will make you know what the payment due is?
Thamūd and ‘Ād called the striking a lie.
As for Thamūd, they were destroyed by the outbreak.

verses.quran_arabic: وَأَمَّا عَادٌۭ فَأُهْلِكُوا۟ بِرِيحٍۢ صَرْصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٍۢ
verses.text: And as for ‘Ād, they were destroyed by a furious, violent wind,

verses.quran_arabic: سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيَالٍۢ وَثَمَٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُومًۭا فَتَرَى ٱلْقَوْمَ فِيهَا صَرْعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُمْ أَعْجَازُ نَخْلٍ خَاوِيَةٍۢ
verses.text: which He forced on them for seven nights and eight days consecutively, and during which you (could) see the people lying flat, as if they were the trunks of collapsed date palms.
Do you see any remnant of them (now)?

verses.quran_arabic: وَجَآءَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَن قَبْلَهُۥ وَٱلْمُؤْتَفِكَٰتُ بِٱلْخَاطِئَةِ
verses.text: And Pharaoh (too) – and those who were before him, and the overturned (cities) – committed sin,

canonical_variants.arabic: وَجَآءَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ وَمَن قِبَلَهُۥ وَاَلۡمُؤۡتَفِكَٰتُ بِالۡخَاطِئَةِ
canonical_variants.english: Then Pharaoh and whoever was on his side and the Overturned Cities came up with grave offenses

canonical_variants.arabic: وَجَآءَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ وَمَن قِبَلَهُۥ وَاَلۡمُوتَفِكَٰتُ بِالۡخَاطِئَةِ
canonical_variants.english: Then Pharaoh and whoever was on his side and the Overturned Cities came up with grave offenses

verses.quran_arabic: فَعَصَوْا۟ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةًۭ رَّابِيَةً
verses.text: and they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a surpassing seizing.

verses.quran_arabic: إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا ٱلْمَآءُ حَمَلْنَٰكُمْ فِى ٱلْجَارِيَةِ
verses.text: Surely We – when the waters overflowed – We carried you in the running (ship),

verses.quran_arabic: لِنَجْعَلَهَا لَكُمْ تَذْكِرَةًۭ وَتَعِيَهَآ أُذُنٌۭ وَٰعِيَةٌۭ
verses.text: so that We might make it a reminder to you, and (that) an attentive ear might attend to it.

verses.quran_arabic: فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ نَفْخَةٌۭ وَٰحِدَةٌۭ
verses.text: When a single blast is blown on the trumpet,

verses.quran_arabic: وَحُمِلَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةًۭ وَٰحِدَةًۭ
verses.text: and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and shattered with a single shattering,
on that Day the falling will fall,
and the sky will be split open, (for) on that Day it will be frail,

verses.quran_arabic: وَٱلْمَلَكُ عَلَىٰٓ أَرْجَآئِهَا ۚ وَيَحْمِلُ عَرْشَ رَبِّكَ فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ ثَمَٰنِيَةٌۭ
verses.text: and the angels (will stand) on its borders, and they will bear the throne of your Lord above them on that Day – eight (of them).

verses.quran_arabic: يَوْمَئِذٍۢ تُعْرَضُونَ لَا تَخْفَىٰ مِنكُمْ خَافِيَةٌۭ
verses.text: On that Day you will (all) be presented – not a secret of yours will be hidden.

verses.quran_arabic: فَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ هَآؤُمُ ٱقْرَءُوا۟ كِتَٰبِيَهْ
verses.text: As for the one who is given his book in his right (hand), he will say, ‘Take (and) read my book.
Surely I thought that I would meet my reckoning.’
And he will be in a pleasing life,
in a Garden on high,
its clusters (of fruit) near (at hand).

verses.quran_arabic: كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَآ أَسْلَفْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَيَّامِ ٱلْخَالِيَةِ
verses.text: ‘Eat and drink with satisfaction, (in return) for what you did in days past.’

verses.quran_arabic: وَأَمَّا مَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَٰبَهُۥ بِشِمَالِهِۦ فَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُوتَ كِتَٰبِيَهْ
verses.text: But as for the one who is given his book in his left (hand), he will say, ‘Would that I had not been given my book,
and not known what my reckoning is!
Would that it had been the end!
My wealth is of no use to me.
My authority has perished from me.’
‘Seize him and bind him!
Then burn him in the Furnace,

verses.quran_arabic: ثُمَّ فِى سِلْسِلَةٍۢ ذَرْعُهَا سَبْعُونَ ذِرَاعًۭا فَٱسْلُكُوهُ
verses.text: (and) then put him in a chain of seventy cubits.

verses.quran_arabic: إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لَا يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ
verses.text: Surely he never believed in God, the Almighty,
nor did he ever urge the feeding of the poor.
So today he has no friend here,
nor any food except refuse,
which only the sinners eat.’
I swear by what you see
and what you do not see!
Surely it is indeed the word of an honorable messenger.

verses.quran_arabic: وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَاعِرٍۢ ۚ قَلِيلًۭا مَّا تُؤْمِنُونَ
verses.text: It is not the word of a poet – little do you believe!

verses.quran_arabic: وَلَا بِقَوْلِ كَاهِنٍۢ ۚ قَلِيلًۭا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
verses.text: Nor (is it) the word of an oracle-giver – little do you take heed!
(It is) a sending down from the Lord of the worlds.
If he had forged any (false) words against Us,
We would indeed have seized him by the right (hand).
Then We would indeed have cut his (main) artery,

verses.quran_arabic: فَمَا مِنكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ عَنْهُ حَٰجِزِينَ
verses.text: and not one of you could have defended him from it.
Surely it is a reminder indeed to the ones who guard (themselves).
Yet surely We indeed know that some of you are calling (it) a lie.
Surely it will be a (cause of) regret indeed to the disbelievers.
Yet surely it is the certain truth indeed.
So glorify the name of your Lord, the Almighty.
